body.purpose: Everything the user reads. Cap prose at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase section markers and stat captions. Never an eyebrow.

mono.purpose: Verbatim strings from the user's spec. Never a stylistic choice.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: The one curve for anything that arrives or travels. Every transition and entrance in the build uses it, at 150ms (hover/colour), 200ms (chevron, step label) or 240ms (entrances).

motion.name: rise-in
motion.purpose: The single entrance. Trace payloads (staggered 50ms), the trace panel, the report card, error banners, the ingest panel. Always motion-safe:, always animating up from the visible end state so a headless or reduced-motion render still shows the content.

motion.name: pulse-status
motion.purpose: The live status dot: trace header during a run, ingest panel during upload. Reduced motion is handled by motion-safe: plus the global floor in globals.css.

motion.name: signal-breathe
motion.purpose: The running node's ring in the agent trace. Same tempo as pulse-status but breathes the ring only, so the solid cyan core never fades — the node under watch must stay legible.

motion.name: draw-check
motion.purpose: Beat one of a node finishing: its check strokes itself in. Static attributes draw it complete; only the animation hides it first.

motion.name: draw-line
motion.value: scaleY 0->1 from origin-top, 360ms ease-out-quart, 120ms delay, fill both
motion.purpose: Beat two of a node finishing: the connector carries the run down to the next node. The 120ms delay is what makes the two beats read as one gesture.

motion.name: bar-grow
motion.value: clip-path inset(0 100% 0 0 round 9999px)->inset(0 0 0 0 round 9999px), 440ms ease-out-quart, 60ms stagger, fill both
motion.purpose: The report's category bars growing to their score. clip-path rather than scaleX so the pill cap keeps its radius, and so no layout property is animated. The overall score deliberately does not animate.

motion.name: ease-unfold / ease-fold
motion.purpose: The agent trace disclosure, and nothing else. grid-template-rows 0fr<->1fr interpolates linearly in fr but maps to pixels as a square (height = H*f^2), so the named curve is squared before it is seen and the two directions need opposite pre-distortions. Measured on the real panel at 25/50/75%: unfold 66/91/98, fold 30/5/0. Applied only when the user toggles; the auto-collapse at completion is deliberately instant.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single loud thing on a view. One per screen, always the obvious next action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #38bdf8; color: #0a0c10; font: 600 0.875rem/1.25rem ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; padding: 10px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0ea5e9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Mode Preset
components.description: A one-click review focus. Selected state is a cyan border plus a 10% cyan wash.
components.css: .ds-mode-preset { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: flex-start; gap: 4px; background: #0f1218; border: 1px solid #1e2530; border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px 14px; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-mode-preset__label { color: #f1f5f9; font: 500 0.875rem/1.25rem ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-mode-preset:hover { background: #161b24; border-color: #2a3340; } .ds-mode-preset:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-mode-preset--active { background: rgba(56, 189, 248, 0.1); border-color: #38bdf8; }

components.name: Question Input
components.description: Free-form review question. Its focus treatment is the best state in the system.
components.html: <textarea class="ds-input-question" rows="2" placeholder="e.g. Are the pagination parameters documented consistently?"></textarea>
components.css: .ds-input-question { width: 100%; resize: none; background: #0f1218; color: #e2e8f0; border: 1px solid #1e2530; border-radius: 12px; padding: 10px 14px; font: 400 0.875rem/1.25rem ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-input-question::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input-question:focus { outline: none; border-color: #38bdf8; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #38bdf8; } .ds-input-question:disabled { opacity: 0.6; }

components.name: Severity Badge
components.description: States an issue's severity. Glyph plus label, so it survives grayscale and color-blindness.
components.html: ✕Critical!Warning✓Pass
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 10px; font: 600 0.75rem/1.333 ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.025em; margin-right: 8px; } .ds-badge--critical { background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.15); color: #ef4444; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.3); } .ds-badge--warning { background: rgba(245, 158, 11, 0.15); color: #f59e0b; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(245, 158, 11, 0.3); } .ds-badge--pass { background: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.15); color: #22c55e; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.3); }

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: The default container. Flat, hairline-bordered, welded to the page plane.
components.html:
Review report

Depth comes from a tonal step, never a shadow.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0f1218; border: 1px solid #1e2530; border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; } .ds-card__title { color: #f1f5f9; font: 600 1.125rem/1.556 ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.025em; margin: 0 0 6px; } .ds-card__body { color: #94a3b8; font: 400 0.875rem/1.43 ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; margin: 0; }

components.name: Dropzone
components.description: The entry point to the whole product. Dashed Edge border is the system's only dashed stroke.
components.html:
Drag & drop your API spec here
or browse to choose a file
components.css: .ds-dropzone { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 12px; background: #0f1218; border: 2px dashed #2a3340; border-radius: 16px; padding: 56px 24px; text-align: center; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-dropzone__title { color: #f1f5f9; font: 500 1rem/1.5 ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-dropzone__hint { color: #94a3b8; font: 400 0.875rem/1.43 ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-dropzone__link { color: #38bdf8; } .ds-dropzone:hover { background: #161b24; border-color: #38bdf8; } .ds-dropzone:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Agent Trace
components.description: The signature component and the evidence for 'shows its work'. Named nodes append live as the stream arrives. Shown here at its current fidelity, which PRODUCT.md flags as the largest strategy-to-build gap.
components.html:
Running agent…
  1. ✓Planning sub-questions
  2. ✓Retrieving relevant sections
  3. ◌Grading chunk relevance
components.css: .ds-trace { background: #0f1218; border: 1px solid #1e2530; border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; } .ds-trace__head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; color: #e2e8f0; font: 500 0.875rem/1.25rem ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; margin-bottom: 10px; } .ds-trace__pulse { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #38bdf8; animation: ds-trace-pulse 2s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite; } .ds-trace__list { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-trace__step { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; color: #94a3b8; font: 400 0.875rem/1.43 ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-trace__glyph { font-size: 0.75rem; } .ds-trace__glyph--done { color: #22c55e; } .ds-trace__glyph--live { color: #38bdf8; } @keyframes ds-trace-pulse { 50% { opacity: 0.4; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-trace__pulse { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview:
This is a calibrated instrument, not a dashboard. An instrument earns trust by reporting exactly what it measures — never louder, never with flourish, never with a number inflated for effect. The visitor is here to judge the quality of the agent's reasoning, and the interface's only job is to make that reasoning legible. Confidence comes from legibility, not decoration. Every surface should feel like it was built by someone who expected to be inspected closely, because that is precisely the audience.

The system is near-black and tonal. Depth is built from a five-step ink ramp (#0a0c10 → #2a3340) and hairline borders rather than shadow, and the single cyan accent is rationed hard. Type is a plain system sans at a tight scale — there is no display face and no font pairing, because a review tool has nothing to say that a well-set 14px sans can't say better. Density is moderate: roomy enough that a first-time visitor isn't repelled, tight enough that it reads as a tool rather than a brochure.

What this system rejects, in its own words: the generic SaaS dashboard — gradient hero metrics, identical icon-heading-text card grids, glassmorphism, big-number theater. And security-vendor threat theater — blood-red alerting, THREAT LEVEL gauges, fear as an aesthetic. Both are live risks here rather than abstractions: this product renders a 0–100 score and a red-amber-green severity triad, which is exactly the raw material each cliché is made from. The discipline is to report severity at the weight it is actually severe.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black, tonally layered; no shadows in the current build
One accent, rationed to state and action only
System sans, single family, tight scale (1.125–1.33 between steps)
Severity communicated by ranking and clarity, never by alarm
Generous radii (8–16px) and roomy padding — approachable, not clinical
Built to survive a close look: real focus states, real contrast, real keyboard paths

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Cyan appears on no more than ~10% of any screen, and only to mean live, focused, selected, or primary action. Its rarity is the entire point — the moment it becomes a decorative fill, the running-node pulse stops meaning anything.

rules.name: The Earned Alarm Rule
rules.body: Severity color is issued by the scorer, never by the designer. Critical red appears on a badge and a bar and nowhere else; it may never wash a card, tint a background at full saturation, or set the page mood. If the interface looks alarming before you've read a single issue title, it is lying about the severity.

rules.name: The Ramp-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth in the current build is a step along the ink ramp, optionally with a Line hairline. A nested surface goes up one step (Surface → Raised); it does not gain a shadow.

rules.name: The Glass Pane Rule
rules.body: Type never performs. No display face, no font pairing, no gradient text, no clamp-scaled headings. If a heading needs more presence, it gets weight or a size step — never a personality.

rules.name: The Literal Mono Rule
rules.body: Monospace means the characters came verbatim out of the user's spec. It is forbidden as a stylistic choice for labels, numbers, or 'technical feel.'

rules.name: The Quiet Label Rule
rules.body: The uppercase 12px label is a section marker, and the system is already close to over-using it. It earns its place where it captions a value or names a block a reader might skip to. It is not an eyebrow: never stack one above every heading as scaffolding.

rules.name: The One Loud Thing Rule
rules.body: Exactly one Signal Cyan primary button per view. If two elements are competing to be the obvious next action, the screen is wrong, not the button.

rules.name: The Honest Disabled Rule
rules.body: A disabled control must read as unavailable at a glance, not as an enabled control behind fog.

dos: Do ration Signal Cyan (#38bdf8) to live state, focus, selection, and the single primary action — the One Voice Rule, ≤10% of any screen.
Do build depth by stepping the ink ramp (Void #0a0c10 → Surface #0f1218 → Raised #161b24) with 1px Line (#1e2530) borders, matching the current build.
Do give the agent trace the design attention normally reserved for the report. It is the evidence for shows its work.
Do copy the textarea's focus treatment (border → Signal Cyan plus a 1px cyan ring) onto every interactive element. It's the system's best state and it's currently used once.
Do pair every severity color with its glyph and label (✕ Critical, ! Warning, ✓ Pass), so severity survives color-blindness and grayscale.
Do hold body text to 4.5:1 against the surface it actually renders on, per PRODUCT.md's WCAG 2.2 AA commitment.
Do keep transitions at 150–250ms, and name a duration and a curve at every call site. A bare `transition` is Tailwind's 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) wearing the system's clothes.

Do reach for one of the four gestures (entrance / live signal / draw / fold) before inventing a fifth. They live in tailwind.config.ts as rise-in, pulse-status + signal-breathe, draw-check + draw-line, and ease-unfold + ease-fold.

Do write animations so the end state is the static one and the animation only hides it first — fill: both plus motion-safe:. Reduced motion, a hidden tab and a headless render then all show finished content instead of nothing. Verify by cancelling every animation (getAnimations().forEach(a => a.cancel())) and checking the page is still complete.

Do say only what is known at the moment you say it. The cold-start notice hedges at 10s and names the cause at 45s because that is when each becomes true, and it stays silent while the analyzer runs because a slow analyzer is Groq, not the embedder. A UI that explains a delay with the wrong reason is worse than one that says nothing — it spends the trust the trace earns.
donts: Don't write a raw slate-* or red-* utility. Every text colour is a token (bright / body / secondary / muted, plus the severity trio). Nine AA failures got in precisely because the roles were named in DESIGN.md but never existed in tailwind.config.ts, so each call site picked its own gray.
Don't darken Muted back past #7c8ba1. It is the floor of the text ramp and sits at 5.21:1 on the lightest surface it lands on; #64748b is one step down and measures 3.94:1.

Don't check contrast against the nominal surface. Composite the whole ancestor chain first, alpha included. The Critical badge reads as #ef4444 on ink-800 (4.98:1, fine) and is actually #ef4444 on rgb(51,29,35) (4.18:1, failing) because its own 15% tint sits between.

Don't trust a timing function on grid-template-rows. Interpolating 0fr<->1fr is linear in fr, but fr maps to pixels as a SQUARE (height = H*f^2), so whatever curve you name gets squared before anyone sees it — out-quart lands as (1-t)^8, a snap with a tail. The squaring is symmetric, so one curve cannot serve both directions: measured with linear, expand runs 6/25/56 and collapse 56/25/6. Measure the pixels, not the curve.

Don't explain infrastructure with severity colour. A cold start, a wake, a dropped connection: none are findings, and the report renders real amber warnings about the user's spec one panel down. The Earned Alarm Rule guards the scorer's palette from the designer; this is the same rule pointed at the other kind of message. Reaching for amber because a notice feels urgent is how the triad stops meaning anything.

Don't add a backdrop-blur to a surface nothing scrolls under. The header carried one for a static bar: a real compositing layer filtering a flat, uniform backdrop into the identical flat colour. Blur is rare and purposeful, or nothing.

Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no gradient hero metric, no identical icon-heading-text card grids, no glassmorphism, no big-number theater. Audit test: the 80px score ring is one gradient and one supporting-stat row away from being the exact cliché PRODUCT.md rejects. If the number is the most designed thing on the page, the reasoning that produced it has been demoted.

Don't drift into security-vendor threat theater: no blood-red alerting, no THREAT LEVEL gauges, no fear as an aesthetic. Audit test: if a low score makes the page feel dangerous rather than report that it's low, back the color out.

Don't nest a card inside a card. The report container currently holds a bordered summary block and bordered issue rows — both are nested cards, and nested cards are always wrong. Use a tonal step to Raised, or a hairline divider, or nothing.
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient. Ever. Weight and size carry emphasis.
Don't use a border-left/border-right over 1px as a colored severity stripe on issue rows. It's the obvious next move for severity and it's banned — the badge already does that job.
Don't introduce a second font family or a display face. The Glass Pane Rule: type never performs.
Don't let the uppercase 12px label become an eyebrow above every section. It captions values and names skippable blocks; it is not scaffolding.
Don't reach for a modal. There are two routes and one flow; anything that wants a modal wants to be inline.
